destinations.evidence: The RPC module constructor accepts an externally-created Arc<SpcVerifier> and stores it in the RPC context instead of constructing an unrelated verifier per request.
The accepted verifier is the one used to gate async result submission.

equivalence_argument: The current module-level Ritual async RPC API is the port equivalent of a RitualApi constructor accepting a shared verifier; the verifier is dependency-injected and reused by registered methods.
notes: Mapped to the current module constructor rather than the older RitualApi symbol.
rationale: The new constructor accepts provider, pool, and Arc<SpcVerifier>, then assigns that verifier directly to the struct field. The comments state this enables sharing a pre-configured verifier across components. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RitualApi::new_with_spc_verifier must store and use the exact Arc<SpcVerifier> provided by the caller, allowing RPC and executor components to share the same verifier instance.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create an Arc<SpcVerifier>, construct RitualApi with new_with_spc_verifier, and assert the stored verifier is Arc::ptr_eq to the supplied instance and is used for verify_result.
summary: The Ritual async RPC module accepts and uses a shared SPC verifier.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create an Arc<SpcVerifier>, construct RitualApi with new_with_spc_verifier, and assert the stored verifier is Arc::ptr_eq to the supplied instance and is used for verify_result.
title: RitualApi accepts shared SPC verifier
